$comment: Template for the @sulesky/opencode-plugger-tui sibling package. The CI release workflow swaps this in as package.json for the second `npm publish` step, then restores the server package.json. Keep `version` / `peerDependencies.@sulesky/opencode-plugger` in lockstep with the main package.json — the workflow does this automatically by reading the server version and templating it in.
name: @sulesky/opencode-plugger-tui
author: Lucjan Sulewski
$comment_exports: OpenCode's plugin loader (`packages/opencode/src/plugin/shared.ts:resolvePackageEntrypoint`) resolves a TUI plugin by reading `exports['./tui']` exclusively — kind='tui' does NOT fall back to `main`. Without this key the npm-installed package errors with 'does not expose a tui entrypoint'.

dependencies.$comment_dependencies: Pinned to the exact server version we publish from the same git tag — keeps the TUI bundle's runtime imports from drifting against a server build that doesn't yet export what it expects. Workflow templates this in.
